success_surface: Complex multi-row data removal with formula recalculation and structural compaction while preserving worksheet integrity
effective_behaviors: Comprehensive exploratory analysis before solution implementation to understand spreadsheet structure, headers, data ranges, and cell relationships
Appropriate tool selection: openpyxl for preserving formulas and structure during read/write, with awareness of pandas limitations
Systematic schema validation including column identification, row counting, label location finding, and proper handling of None/empty values
Frequency-aware data filtering that removes only one instance per matching value rather than eliminating all duplicates
Manual formula recalculation after data modifications since saved files may not auto-update Excel formulas
Post-write verification by reloading the output file to confirm changes were applied correctly
Compact data reorganization maintaining contiguous rows without gaps after removals
evidence: Agent executed 3 exploration iterations extracting structure, column values, target location before implementing solution
Used load_workbook() with openpyxl to access cell values while being aware pandas.read_excel converts formulas to static values
Built frequency map of result values and decremented count when removing matches: result_count[rv] -= 1
Manually calculated Solver Result (sumproduct) and Difference values, wrote them directly instead of relying on =SUMPRODUCT formula
After wb.save(), reloaded verification_wb and checked Header Row, Data Section, Formula cells before completing
Remaining data written starting from correct offset (output_offset=2) ensuring no empty rows between header and data

memory_items.title: Exploratory Pre-Solution Analysis
memory_items.lesson: Before modifying spreadsheets, exhaustively explore structure, headers, data types, and key cell locations through targeted queries
memory_items.generalization: Always validate worksheet schema, identify formula-containing cells, locate relevant labels/ranges, and handle None values explicitly before implementing modifications

memory_items.title: Tool Selection Based on Preservation Requirements
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for workbooks requiring formula/style preservation; understand pandas converts formulas to static values during load
memory_items.generalization: Match tool choice to task needs: openpyxl for structural fidelity, pandas for computation-heavy transformations without formula concerns

memory_items.title: Frequency-Aware Value Deduplication
memory_items.lesson: When removing matching values, track occurrence counts to remove exactly the required number of instances
memory_items.generalization: Build frequency maps for target values and decrement counters on each match to avoid over-removal or under-removal

memory_items.title: Manual Formula Recalculation Post-Modification
memory_items.lesson: After changing data that feeds formulas, manually calculate and write results since saved .xlsx files won't auto-recompute
memory_items.generalization: Identify all dependent formula cells, compute their new values programmatically, and write explicit values rather than relying on Excel engine

memory_items.title: Output Verification Through Reload
memory_items.lesson: Always reload the saved output file to verify modifications before considering the task complete
memory_items.generalization: Implement verification loops that load the output, spot-check critical cells/ranges, and confirm formulas/values as expected

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Exploration Before Modification
- Always perform comprehensive exploratory analysis of spreadsheet structure (headers, data ranges, formula locations) before implementing solutions
- Validate schema, identify cell relationships, and handle edge cases (None values, empty rows) through targeted queries

### Tool Selection Strategy
- Use `openpyxl` for workbooks requiring formula/style preservation; `pandas.read_excel()` converts formulas to static values
- Match tool choice to task requirements: structural fidelity vs. computational efficiency

### Deduplication with Count Tracking
- When removing matching values, build frequency maps to ensure exactly the required number of instances are removed
- Decrement counters on each match to prevent over-removal or under-removal scenarios

### Formula Recalculation Protocol
- After data modifications affecting formulas, manually compute and write results programmatically
- Saved `.xlsx` files do not auto-recompute; explicit value writes ensure correct downstream calculations

### Output Verification Loop
- Implement mandatory verification by reloading the saved output file
- Spot-check critical cells, ranges, and formulas against expected values before task completion

### Compact Data Reorganization
- When removing rows/items, maintain contiguous data without gaps
- Write remaining data from correct offsets ensuring proper alignment with headers
